channel.title: maya's blog
channel.description: maya's blog at the end of the universe.

item.title: making this blog less of a pain to write to
item.description: the tale of a full technology migration so i don't have to edit 12 files to publish a single blogpost

item.title: reflecting on work
item.description: it has been about a year since leaving my employer, and i'm not too sure how i feel about that

item.title: what the heck is an uwu-space
item.description: how i may have ended co-founding a hackspace in zurich, and helped stand up the infrastructure backbone

item.title: train ticket shenanigans: the 83rd kontiki conference
item.description: the german public transport authorities invited us to talk about train tickets this time, i went to hamburg with an extra mission: co-delivering a talk about train tickets

item.title: a 39th congress. this time with extra chaos.
item.description: this time, i went to hamburg with an extra mission: co-delivering a talk about train tickets

item.title: once more to BSides London
item.description: going to bsides london to help out and hang out

item.title: things i've been up to
item.description: just a rapid fire series of small updates

item.title: returning this silly little blog to the era of "small internet"
item.description: maya is making things less "big tech"

item.title: WHY2025, a Dutch hacker camp experience
item.description: once more, maya was in a field, and somehow helped the internet spawn there

item.title: a semester in review, what even is catgirl dot global
item.description: going over some of the shenanigans i have been up to in the last few months :)

item.title: going to montreal on holidays
item.description: i went to montreal to spend some time somewhere very different, and to revisit my past

item.title: going to Hack Glasgow, a new conference up in the west of Scotland.
item.description: i went to glasgow to help out some friends with their new conference :)

item.title: maya does an infrastruktur!
item.description: maya has decided that it should make its internet closer to home, maybe in a closet

item.title: maya went to 38c3!
item.description: maya goes to congress again, volunteers a bit (this surp... this joke is getting old), and has a fun time.

item.title: bsides london, 0% attendance run (sorry)
item.description: I went to a conference and did some volunteering! (this surprises no one)

item.title: Doing conference A/V shenanigans at BSides NCL.
item.description: I went to a conference and got given things to do! (this surprises no one)

item.title: New experiences at DEFCON 32
item.description: I went to DEFCON32 and volunteered.

item.title: Maya goes on holidays in the Pacific Northwest
item.description: Maya decided to go on a pre-DEFCON visit to the US West Coast.

item.title: Startup Whiplash! At Steelcon!
item.description: One of my rants, this time with extra punch, sure to give whiplash.

item.title: Electromagnetic Fields!
item.description: I went to a field near Ledbury. Oh, and so did 3000 or so other hackers!

item.title: Whoops! That's some PII!
item.description: I stumbled onto the database of a lumber/building materials buying group in Canada. A tale of responsible disclosure.

item.title: New Year, New Stuff!
item.description: A few changes to this blog.

item.title: Attending Chaos Congress, a highlight to close off the year.
item.description: Chaos Congress, the perfect place for this chaos goose to end the year.

item.title: I somehow spoke at BSides London! (an unscheduled development)
item.description: BSides London! (I failed the "Maya don't speak at a BSides challenge" again)

item.title: Making this blog easier to deploy!
item.description: Blogging, on the move?

item.title: It's been 3 years.
item.description: 3 years, 54 blog posts, a bit of a brain dump.

item.title: Attending BoncleSides!
item.description: I went to Newcastle upon Tyne to attend and speak at a conference. I ended up also winning second place at the CTF.

item.title: I went to C3Camp!
item.description: Chaos Computer Camp, or how a bunch of hackers and makers in a field in Germany make for quite a cool event!

item.title: I went to DEFCON31!
item.description: I have once more been graced with the opportunity to head to Vegas, for one of the worlds' largest hacker conferences.

item.title: JavaScript war crimes! At SteelCon.
item.description: Maya is back - with a vengeance - to teach people about JSFuck.

item.title: A Phishing Expedition!
item.description: I somehow got involved in analyzing a phishing kit that was making the rounds... So let's talk about its technical aspects.

item.title: Yet another new job.
item.description: I have a new job, and am leaving academia.

item.title: Revamping my Bachelor's project
item.description: I made a program to visualize Dominant Language Constellations in 3D, to digitize a process done until then with sticks and papier maché

item.title: My gender paradigm shift.
item.description: Gender identity! A fun topic, and my personal stake in it.

item.title: I went to Wales for BSides Cymru!
item.description: BSides Cymru, or why I went to hang out with a bunch of trans and queer folk in Wales

item.title: I finished my Master's degree!
item.description: Explaining the conspicuous disappearance of my Internet self. (ish)

item.title: Heading out to BSides London!
item.description: BSides London, one of the UK's biggest BSides events, and the friends that were made along the way.

item.title: I went to South Korea!
item.description: A big university trip halfway around the world.

item.title: Heading out to DTX360 and BeerCon4!
item.description: BeerCon4, a cybersecurity event! (but at a trade show???)

item.title: I went to Scotland!
item.description: I attended a wedding in Scotland, and met up with some of my friends!

item.title: I spoke at DEFCON30! (I also attended it!)
item.description: DEFCON30! All the shenanigans, all the hackers, all the bad luck?

item.title: Going back to Amsterdam and attending MCH!
item.description: I attended MayContainHackers 2022, and got into some shenanigans!

item.title: Going to Paris for the Wavegame cloud competition!
item.description: I went to Paris for the WaveGame competition, and we ended up winning!

item.title: Building a Raspberry Pi private web server!
item.description: Going behind the scenes on developping a private web service deployed on a Raspberry Pi.

item.title: Digital Overdose Conference 2: We're doing this again!
item.description: Going behind the scenes of the second iteration of the Digital Overdose Conference.

item.title: I started a new job!
item.description: Going into a bit more detail about what I'm currently working on!

item.title: Dissecting the KnightCTF Cryptography challenges!
item.description: KnightCTF! A write-up on solving challenges from the "Cryptography" category.

item.title: Dissecting the KnightCTF Misc challenges!
item.description: KnightCTF! A write-up on solving challenges from the "Misc" category.

item.title: Dissecting the KnightCTF Programming challenges!
item.description: KnightCTF! A write-up on solving challenges from the "Programming" category.

item.title: Dissecting the KnightCTF Reverse Engineering challenges!
item.description: KnightCTF! A write-up on solving challenges from the "Reverse Engineering" category.

item.title: Participating in KnightCTF!
item.description: I participated in KnightCTF alongside the team from Digital Overdose, wherein I successfully beat a few challenges.

item.title: Speaking about communities at conINT
item.description: I took a stab at taking one year of experience in managing a community of hackers, and trying to discuss the benefits of Intelligence when it comes to keeping its members safe.

item.title: Happy birthday to this blog!
item.description: Marking the first year of this blog's existence.

item.title: Speaking at BeerCon 3!
item.description: I spoke at BeerCon 3, mainly about how JavaScript and Application Security don't really mesh well together.

item.title: Digital Overdose CTF Official Writeup #1 - Outage
item.description: Official challenge developer writeup for "Outage - The usual suspect", a challenge presented at the Digital Overdose 2021 Autumn CTF.

item.title: Digital Overdose CTF Official Writeup #2 - A door by any other name
item.description: Official challenge developer writeup for "A door by any other name", a challenge presented at the Digital Overdose 2021 Autumn CTF.

item.title: Digital Overdose CTF Official Writeup #3 - C1 - I think this should be C4
item.description: Official challenge developer writeup for"C1 - I think this should be C4", a challenge presented at the Digital Overdose 2021 Autumn CTF.

item.title: Enumerating the Lightshot CDN
item.description: How I went from the premise of an open CDN to exploiting it as a proof of concept

item.title: Halfway through 2021: A brief recap of what I've been up to
item.description: Going over the timeline for the last few months, seeing what has been accomplished.

item.title: The 2021 Digital Overdose Conference: Tales and Takeaways
item.description: Speaking a bit more about my experiences surrounding and during the Digital Overdose Conference.

item.title: Participating in the Central-Infosec CTF!
item.description: The Central Infosec CTF! I didn't do much of it, but here's my takeaways.

item.title: Dissecting the Central-Infosec Game Exploitation challenge
item.description: The Central Infosec CTF! A write-up on solving the "Hack The Maze: Hard" challenge.

item.title: Dissecting the Central-Infosec Static Code Analysis challenge
item.description: The Central Infosec CTF! A write-up on solving the "Hack The Static: Hard" challenge.

item.title: Dissecting the Central-Infosec Python challenges
item.description: The Central Infosec CTF! A write-up on solving the Python challenges.

item.title: A much ado about passwords and password management
item.description: Going over various (non-)solutions people use to store their passwords and the appeal of password management solutions.

item.title: Participating in the TraceLabs OSINT CTF with HackSouth
item.description: A write-up on my participation in the TraceLabs OSINT CTF.

item.title: Building DO's website in 6 days!
item.description: Going through the various steps of how I helped build DO's website.

item.title: Introducing... Digital Overdose Con!
item.description: I'm co-organizing a conference for rookie speakers

item.title: Website Design Choices
item.description: Going over some of my website design choices.

item.title: Talking at Leeds EHS
item.description: The Leeds Ethical Hacking Society hosted my take on ENISA's recommendations for IoT cybersecurity.

item.title: WhatsApp Privacy Policy Changes eNCA Interview
item.description: Somehow the WhatsApp discussion got me interviewed by eNCA

item.title: WhatsApp Privacy Policy Changes Discussion at Hack South
item.description: Dissecting the new WhatsApp privacy policy changes, live on HackSouth.

item.title: WhatsApp Privacy Policy Changes Analysis
item.description: Dissecting the new WhatsApp privacy policy changes.

item.title: Talking about OSINT on Gateway
item.description: I gave a small talk about OSINT on Gateway.

item.title: Mental Health
item.description: Discussing mental health.

item.title: Goodbye 2020: New Year Party
item.description: Saying goodbye to 2020, with friends!

item.title: Security CoffeeHouse: BC2 Speaker Interviews
item.description: I participated in James Bore's Security CoffeeHouse!

item.title: Mentoring!
item.description: I want to help you with your endeavors.

item.title: Reading Materials!
item.description: Some books I plan on reading.

item.title: I talked at BSides Islamabad!
item.description: I gave a talk at BSides Islamabad, Pakistan's first cybersecurity conference.

item.title: I gave a talk at BeerCon2!
item.description: I gave a talk at BeerCon2, a rookie infosec conference.

item.title: I participated in the conINT CTF!
item.description: Discussing my participation in the conINT TraceLabs OSINT capture the flag event.

item.title: Hello World!
item.description: The first post on AtomicMaya's Blog. Wow!
